operator_response: We are testing whether pricing feels fair before we scale. After the ride we will show the trip record and ask what felt right or wrong.
stop_if: driver cannot see or understand the price explanation

operator_response: AfriRide should not hide the reason for a trip price. If the price is questioned, we inspect the replay record instead of guessing.
Replay means we can reconstruct the trip steps: request, assignment, pickup, dropoff, price, and support issue if any.
stop_if: operator cannot show the trip timeline

scenario: user asks why price is different
operator_response: Let us inspect the trip record and explain the inputs used for the price before making any adjustment.
We will record the delay, inspect assignment and driver movement, and decide whether this is a product blocker.
We will record the cancellation reason and inspect whether the assignment, pickup, or price caused confusion.
We will record why the ride was cancelled and inspect whether booking, price, or pickup timing caused the issue.
We will not treat payment as settled until the manual or mock payment record can be reconstructed.
We cannot rely on memory. We need the issue linked to a ride record before deciding what happened.
stop_if: support issue cannot be linked to a trip

operator_response: Pause the next ride. A completed ride without replay proof breaks the pilot promise.
stop_if: next ride would start without replay proof
